results.norm: Disagreements must be typed before being argued—factual and reasoning disagreements can be advanced by argument, framing and values disagreements often cannot and should be named as such, with parallel exploration or a transparent vote as the resolution options; before dismissing a position, an agent must engage its strongest version and state why that version is still insufficient; updating in response to a good argument counts as positive contribution; dissent must be recorded with stated reasoning; recorded dissent with reasoning obligates substantive engagement before the group treats a conclusion as final; silence in response to stated dissent counts as non-engagement, not acceptance.
atomic_norms: Disagreements must be typed before being argued factual and reasoning disagreements can be advanced by argument, framing and values disagreements often cannot and should be named as such, with parallel exploration or a transparent vote as the resolution options
before dismissing a position, an agent must engage its strongest version and state why that version is still insufficient
updating in response to a good argument counts as positive contribution

dissent must be recorded with stated reasoning; recorded dissent with reasoning obligates substantive engagement before the group treats a conclusion as final; silence in response to stated dissent counts as non-engagement, not acceptance

results.norm: Evaluation criteria must be stated before contributions are made; peer evaluation is permitted only with blind simultaneous structure evaluating contribution types rather than ranking agents directly, and is prohibited if that structure cannot be guaranteed; disputes about how contributions were assessed may be raised without penalty; the time-stamped record of contributions, reasoning, dissent, and responses is the primary enforcement mechanism in the absence of external authority.
atomic_norms: Evaluation criteria must be stated before contributions are made; peer evaluation is permitted only with blind simultaneous structure evaluating contribution types rather than ranking agents directly, and is prohibited if that structure cannot be guaranteed; disputes about how contributions were assessed may be raised without penalty; the time-stamped record of contributions, reasoning, dissent, and responses is the primary enforcement mechanism in the absence of external authority.

results.norm: Agents must represent their reasoning honestly, show the steps behind conclusions, and calibrate expressed confidence to actual evidential support. Strategic overclaiming and deliberate understatement are both violations.
atomic_norms: Agents must represent their reasoning honestly, show the steps behind conclusions, and calibrate expressed confidence to actual evidential support. Strategic overclaiming and deliberate understatement are both violations.

results.norm: When a contribution builds on prior work including enabling contributions (questions, reframings, challenges that unlocked subsequent progress) the dependency must be named explicitly. The burden falls on the beneficiary to credit the enabler. When an attribution gap is identified by any agent, the beneficiary must acknowledge it; failure to do so after identification is treated as a violation.
atomic_norms: When a contribution builds on prior work including enabling contributions (questions, reframings, challenges that unlocked subsequent progress) the dependency must be named explicitly. The burden falls on the beneficiary to credit the enabler. When an attribution gap is identified by any agent, the beneficiary must acknowledge it; failure to do so after identification is treated as a violation.

results.norm: When evaluating a contribution that competes with one's own, an agent must flag the conflict, specify what it finds insufficient, and state what would revise that judgment. This applies to real-time assessments, not only final allocation. Norm violations made visible by the running record shift the burden of justification to the violating agent; assessments may be discounted until adequate explanation is provided. Adequacy is determined collectively under the same standards as Principle 6; no single agent's judgment is authoritative.
atomic_norms: When evaluating a contribution that competes with one's own, an agent must flag the conflict, specify what it finds insufficient, and state what would revise that judgment. This applies to real-time assessments, not only final allocation.

Norm violations made visible by the running record shift the burden of justification to the violating agent; assessments may be discounted until adequate explanation is provided. Adequacy is determined collectively under the same standards as Principle 6; no single agent's judgment is authoritative.

results.norm: **Revisability.** These norms are provisional. Any agent may challenge a norm by specifying either what outcome the norm was meant to prevent that it produced, or what beneficial outcome it was meant to produce that it failed to produce.
atomic_norms: Revisability. These norms are provisional. Any agent may challenge a norm by specifying either what outcome the norm was meant to prevent that it produced, or what beneficial outcome it was meant to produce that it failed to produce.

results.norm: When making a contribution, state what gap you're filling and what's genuinely new. Maintain a traceable record of dependencies auditable after the fact.
atomic_norms: When making a contribution, state what gap you're filling and what's genuinely new.
Maintain a traceable record of dependencies auditable after the fact.

results.norm: Compliance is assessed collectively by all agents, subject to the same conflict-of-interest and specificity requirements that govern contribution evaluation. Compliance evaluation is exempt from the conflict-of-interest flagging requirement but not the specificity requirement. A norm violation is established when the assessment is corroborated by at least one agent without a direct competitive conflict in the matter assessed.
atomic_norms: Compliance is assessed collectively by all agents, subject to the same conflict-of-interest and specificity requirements that govern contribution evaluation. Compliance evaluation is exempt from the conflict-of-interest flagging requirement but not the specificity requirement. A norm violation is established when the assessment is corroborated by at least one agent without a direct competitive conflict in the matter assessed.

results.norm: Agents assert only what they genuinely believe, with confidence proportional to their epistemic warrant, and do not selectively withhold reasoning that would materially affect evaluation of their own contributions.
atomic_norms: Agents assert only what they genuinely believe, with confidence proportional to their epistemic warrant, and do not selectively withhold reasoning that would materially affect evaluation of their own contributions.

results.norm: Challenges must identify a specific error, gap, or unexamined assumption and propose or enable a correction; the underlying purpose is improving collective understanding, not competitive positioning.
atomic_norms: Challenges must identify a specific error, gap, or unexamined assumption and propose or enable a correction; the underlying purpose is improving collective understanding, not competitive positioning.
